{"society_power": 7.0, "state_power": 4.5, "society_change": 0.5, "state_change": 0.5, "key_event": "Peaceful transfer of power (1800) and Louisiana Purchase", "reasoning": "Society strengthened through the 1800 electoral turnover, repeal of the Sedition Acts, broadening franchise, and a growing partisan press, though slavery's entrenchment capped gains; the state saw modest net growth as the Louisiana Purchase, judicial review, and embargo enforcement expanded reach even as Jeffersonian fiscal retrenchment and states'-rights nullification claims pulled the other way."}
